title: NA
text: mizerMR R extension package mizer adds support multiple size-structured background resource spectra. enables species feed selectively across distinct resource types (e.g., plankton vs. benthic invertebrates), supporting ontogenetic dietary shifts.

title: NA
text: run within R session via Rscript: Visual regression tests use vdiffr. update snapshots intentional plot changes:
code: # Load package for interactive development devtools::load_all()  # Run all tests devtools::test()  # Run a single test file testthat::test_file("tests/testthat/test-newMRParams.R")  # Rebuild documentation (after editing roxygen comments) devtools::document()  # Full package check devtools::check()  # Install dependencies devtools::install_deps() vdiffr::manage_cases()

title: NA
text: MR-specific state stored params@other_params[["MR"]] — list attached mizer’s MizerParams object. Key sub-slots: $resource_params: data frame one row per resource (columns: resource, kappa, lambda, r_pp, w_min, w_max) $resource_capacity: array [resource × size] — carrying capacity $resource_rate: array [resource × size] — growth/renewal rate $resource_interaction: matrix [species × resource] — feeding preferences $n_pp: initial resource abundances Simulation output stored sim@[["MR"]] — array [time × resource × size], accessed via NResource(sim).

title: NA
text: Setup (setMultipleResources.R, newMRParams.R): User calls setMultipleResources(params, resource_params) newMRParams(...). functions validate inputs, compute size grids per resource, populate other_params[["MR"]], register custom dynamics/encounter functions mizer’s hook system. Projection (resource_dynamics.R, project_methods.R): project(), mizer calls mizerMR_dynamics() (registered other_dynamics[["MR"]]), updates resource using semi-chemostat dynamics. Encounter rates computed mizerMREncounter(), replaces mizer’s default encounter function. Results access (NResource.R, plotResource.R, plotSpectra.R, diet.R): Users retrieve results via NResource(sim) visualise plotSpectra(), animateSpectra(), plotResourceLevel(), plotDiet().

title: NA
text: Requires mizer ≥ 2.4.0 mizerExperimental ≥ 2.4.0 dependencies. package replaces mizer’s single background resource — setMultipleResources() called, original n_pp slot longer used dynamics. Resource size grids independent per resource (w_min/w_max), unlike mizer’s single shared grid. Parameter validation handled validResourceParams() valid_resources_arg() resource_params.R.

title: How mizerMR uses mizer's extension mechanism
text: vignette aimed developers want understand mizerMR built top mizer’s extension machinery. assumes read vignette("extensions", package = "mizer"). mizerMR uses setComponent() multiple-resource state, other_params() resource metadata, mizer’s extension-chain S3 methods mizer generics need know multiple resources. methods call NextMethod() mizerMR can compose extension packages, therMizer, instead replacing entire mizer rate functions plotting helpers.

what: The resource state lives in a setComponent() component
title: How mizerMR uses mizer's extension mechanism
text: multiple resources stored single dynamical component named "MR". component state array dimensions (resource × size) holding number density resource size mizer’s full size grid. setMultipleResources() registers component: call project(), mizer automatically calls mizerMR_dynamics() time step. function iterates resources applies semi-chemostat dynamics row state array, using per-resource mortality computed earlier rate pipeline. component state every saved time step ends sim@n_other[, "MR"] exposed users via NResource(sim) finalNResource(sim).
code: params <- setComponent(     params      = params,     component   = "MR",     initial_value = initial_resource,   # array (resource × size)     dynamics_fun  = "mizerMR_dynamics",     component_params = list(         rate        = resource_rate,        # array (resource × size)         capacity    = resource_capacity,    # array (resource × size)         interaction = resource_interaction  # matrix (species × resource)     ) )

what: mizerMR registers as a dispatching extension
title: How mizerMR uses mizer's extension mechanism
text: mizerMR defines S4 marker classes mizerMR mizerMRSim. package registers mizer loaded: setMultipleResources() finished setting component state, records current session extension chain object coerces object appropriate dispatch class: two-step constructor pattern described mizer’s extension package vignette. stored value mizerMR package requirement string "sizespectrum/mizerMR", mizer can use loading saved objects.
code: .onLoad <- function(libname, pkgname) {     mizer::registerExtensions(         stats::setNames("sizespectrum/mizerMR", pkgname)) } params@extensions <- mizer::getRegisteredExtensions() mizer::coerceToExtensionClass(params)

title: How mizerMR uses mizer's extension mechanism
text: built-mizer encounter resource-mortality rate functions work single n_pp vector. mizerMR adds methods mizerMR extension class extension-aware projections understand multi-resource array.

previous_headings: How mizerMR currently works > Projection methods extend the defaults
title: How mizerMR uses mizer's extension mechanism
text: method first calls NextMethod() get encounter rate rest extension chain (fish community plus silenced built-resource, together extensions’ contributions). adds encounter multiple resources. encounter convolution linear prey spectra, resources share mizer’s full size grid. contributions individual resources need separate Fourier transform : can summed single combined prey spectrum predator transformed . species × resource interaction matrix Θ\Theta resource state NN (array one row per resource), ∑rFFT(Θ⋅rNr)=FFT(ΘN), \sum_r \mathrm{FFT}\bigl(\Theta_{\cdot r}\,N_r\bigr)    = \mathrm{FFT}\bigl(\Theta\,N\bigr),  single transform interaction %*% n_other[["MR"]] gives whole resource encounter. mizerMRResourceEncounter() computes, makes encounter cost flat number resources rather growing . projectEncounter.mizerMR uses fast path whenever model uses mizer’s default Fourier predation kernel: per-resource fallback loop kept models custom, non-Fourier predation kernel, mizerMRResourceEncounter() handle. loop resource exposed turn standard n_pp interaction_resource arguments NextMethod() called , extension methods — example therMizer’s temperature-scaling search volume — still affect multiple-resource encounter contribution. (other_encounter ext_encounter contributions already encounter first NextMethod() call, silenced inside loop avoid counting per resource.) exported mizerMREncounter() function retained compatibility wrapper around mizerMRResourceEncounter(), setMultipleResources() longer installs params@rates_funcs.
code: projectEncounter.mizerMR <- function(params, n, n_pp, n_other, t = 0, ...) {     n_pp <- mizerMRValidBaseResource(params, n_pp)     encounter <- NextMethod(n = n, n_pp = n_pp, n_other = n_other, t = t)     n_mr <- n_other[["MR"]]     if (is.null(n_mr)) return(encounter)      # Fast path: one FFT for all resources combined.     if (is.null(comment(params@pred_kernel))) {         return(encounter + mizerMRResourceEncounter(params, n_other))     }      # Fallback for a user-supplied (non-Fourier) predation kernel: add each     # resource's contribution separately by exposing it through the standard     # `n_pp` and `interaction_resource` arguments and calling `NextMethod()`.     # ... (per-resource loop, see source) ... }

previous_headings: How mizerMR currently works > Projection methods extend the defaults
title: How mizerMR uses mizer's extension mechanism
text: mizer’s default ResourceMort returns single vector (one value per size bin) representing mortality n_pp. mizerMR method returns matrix (resource × size) multiplying predation rate matrix species–resource interaction: NextMethod() call keeps extension chain live. mizerMR use single-resource mortality vector returned mizer’s base method; instead returns matrix later passed row--row per-resource dynamics function inside mizerMR_dynamics().
code: projectResourceMort.mizerMR <- function(params, n, n_pp, n_other, t,                                         pred_rate, ...) {     NextMethod()     t(params@other_params[["MR"]]$interaction) %*% pred_rate }

what: The built-in single resource is silenced
title: How mizerMR uses mizer's extension mechanism
text: resource dynamics now handled “MR” component, built-mizer resource must interfere. setMultipleResources() two things achieve :
code: mizer::initialNResource(params) <- 0         # abundance set to zero resource_dynamics(params) <- "resource_constant"  # kept at zero forever

what: Resource parameters live in two places
title: How mizerMR uses mizer's extension mechanism
text: Resource-level parameters (kappa, lambda, r_pp, w_min, w_max) stored data frame other_params(params)[["MR"]]$resource_params. per-size arrays derived parameters (carrying capacity replenishment rate) live inside component’s component_params. split reflects two-level structure recommended mizer: other_params() model-wide state, component_params data belonging single component.

what: Accessors, plots and species utilities are methods
title: How mizerMR uses mizer's extension mechanism
text: Functions like NResource(), finalNResource(), plotSpectra(), getDiet(), plotDiet() need behave differently depending whether model multiple resources . mizer S3 generics, mizerMR registers methods mizerMR mizerMRSim objects use multiple-resource component. methods still call NextMethod() extension packages can participate generic. user calls NResource(sim) sim mizerMRSim, R dispatches NResource.mizerMRSim. sim plain MizerSim (multiple resources), R dispatches mizer’s NResource.MizerSim. pattern applies finalNResource, plotSpectra, getDiet, plotDiet, animateSpectra. Species-manipulation helpers follow rule. Methods addSpecies.mizerMR, removeSpecies.mizerMR, renameSpecies.mizerMR expandSizeGrid.mizerMR strip MR component temporarily mizer’s base operation needs run ordinary single-resource object, call NextMethod(), rebuild MR component returned object.
code: NResource.mizerMRSim <- function(sim) {     NextMethod()     n_res <- aperm(simplify2array(NOther(sim)[, "MR"]), c(3, 1, 2))     dimnames(n_res)[[1]] <- dimnames(NOther(sim))[[1]]     names(dimnames(n_res))[[1]] <- names(dimnames(NOther(sim)))[[1]]     n_res }

what: Model rescaling and reporting are methods
title: How mizerMR uses mizer's extension mechanism
text: mizer functions transform report whole model also need aware resources, functions know single built-resource mizerMR silenced. mizerMR therefore provides methods : scaleModel.mizerMR scaleRates.mizerMR rescale resource capacities, abundances rates stored MR component, base methods see (base scaleModel() even error mizerMR object). delegate consumer scaling base method. setResource.mizerMR warns affects silenced built-resource points user setMultipleResources() resource_rate<- / resource_capacity<- setters. summary.mizerMR reports combined size range resources instead (empty) built-resource. scaleModel.mizerMR setResource.mizerMR reach base method coercing plain class extension chain temporarily cleared, base method’s internal validParams() re-promote object mizerMR class dispatch back MR-aware setters. project() returns mizerMRSim object automatically params object inside simulation carries mizerMR extension chain, correct methods fire without user anything special.

title: How mizerMR uses mizer's extension mechanism
text: extension mechanism mizer designed kind layering mind. Using methods projection hooks, accessors plots keeps mizerMR composable extension packages makes codebase easier maintain.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Gustav Delius. Maintainer, author. Romain Forestier. Author.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Delius G, Forestier R (2026). mizerMR: Add multiple size-structured resources mizer model. R package version 0.3.0.0, https://sizespectrum.org/mizerMR.
code: @Manual{,   title = {mizerMR: Add multiple size-structured resources to a mizer model},   author = {Gustav Delius and Romain Forestier},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.3.0.0},   url = {https://sizespectrum.org/mizerMR}, }

title: Multiple size-structured resources in mizer
text: extension package mizer allows work multiple size-structured background resources way work multiple species mizer. Modelled species can different preferences different resources, defined though maximum availability resource available species, similar way setting species interaction matrix. background resource can different minimum maximum sizes, different size spectrum slopes (lambda) abundances (kappa). allows user reproduce emergent onto-genetic dietary shifts, species feed plankton spectrum small, switches benthic spectrum, later fish species. uses mizer’s extension chain projection-rate hooks, models multiple resources can composed extension packages therMizer.

title: Multiple size-structured resources in mizer
code: remotes::install_github("sizespectrum/mizerMR") library(mizerMR)

what: Setting up a model with multiple resources
title: Multiple size-structured resources in mizer
text: Multiple size-structured resources set similar manner multiple species. Just multiple species need species_params data frame information species, multiple resources need resource_params data frame, one row resource. artificial example two resources: Similarly can set resource parameters single resource standard mizer setResource(), can now set multiple resources setMultipleResources(). example wanted add resources North Sea model described NS_params MizerParams object, given us new MizerParams object original resource replaced two resources. can work MizerParams object usual. example can project forward time: plot resulting spectra:  can also animate spectra animateSpectra(sim). mizer functions work usual. can access simulation results resource NResource(). gives three-dimensional array: first dimension time, second resource third size. Thus get abundance density Resource 2 time 0.6 sizes 0.1g 5g perfectly analogous access abundance density fish species, except range sizes given w_full rather w.
code: library(tibble) resource_params <- tribble(     ~resource,  ~kappa, ~lambda, ~r_pp, ~w_min, ~w_max,  "Resource 1",    1e11,    2.13,     4,    NA ,   0.01,  "Resource 2",    1e11,    2.05,    10,   1e-4,     NA ) params <- setMultipleResources(NS_params, resource_params) sim <- project(params, t_max = 2, t_save = 0.2) plotSpectra(sim, power = 2) str(NResource(sim)) #>  num [1:11, 1:2, 1:226] 9.87e+37 9.87e+37 9.87e+37 9.87e+37 9.87e+37 ... #>  - attr(*, "dimnames")=List of 3 #>   ..$ time    : chr [1:11] "0" "0.2" "0.4" "0.6" ... #>   ..$ resource: chr [1:2] "Resource 1" "Resource 2" #>   ..$ w       : chr [1:226] "2.12e-13" "2.53e-13" "3.02e-13" "3.61e-13" ... select_w <- w_full(params) >= 1 & w_full(params) <= 5 NResource(sim)["0.6", "Resource 2", select_w] #>        1.18         1.4        1.68           2        2.39        2.85  #> 29171099375 20197414266 14020889066  9759285393  6811166565  4765970415  #>         3.4        4.06        4.84  #>  3342985300  2349927216  1654807045

title: Multiple size-structured resources in mizer
text: Just able specify strongly predator species interacts prey species via interaction matrix, can also specify strongly predator species interacts resource via resource_interaction, matrix one row species one column resource. example Resource parameters can changed time. example can double carrying capacity first resource can also use kind notation alternative setMultispeciesParams(). example default initial values resources equal carrying capacities. user array (resource x size) resource number densities my_n_resource can set initial values Users happy allometric expressions resource carrying capacities replenishment rates created matrices my_capacity my_rate one row resource one column size can set Rather adding multiple resources existing model, user can also create new model multiple resources
code: resource_interaction <- matrix(runif(24), nrow = 12, ncol = 2) params <- setMultipleResources(NS_params, resource_params, resource_interaction) resource_params(params)$kappa[1] <- 2 * resource_params(params)$kappa[1] params <- NS_params resource_params(params) <- resource_params resource_interaction(params) <- resource_interaction initialNResource(params) <- my_n_resource resource_capacity(params) <- my_capacity resource_rate(params) <- my_rate params <- newMRParams(species_params, interaction, resource_params,                       resource_interaction, gear_params)

title: Get diet of predator at size, resolved by prey species — getDiet
text: params MizerParams object n matrix species abundances (species x size). n_pp vector resource abundance size n_other list abundances dynamical components proportion TRUE (default) function returns diet proportion total consumption rate. FALSE returns consumption rate grams per year.

title: Get diet of predator at size, resolved by prey species — getDiet
text: array (predator species  x predator size x (prey species + resource + components) )

title: Get diet of predator at size, resolved by prey species — getDiet
text: Returns rates \(D_{ij}(w)\) predator species \(\) size \(w\) consumes biomass prey species \(j\). calculated predation kernel \(\phi_i(w, w_p)\), search volume \(\gamma_i(w)\), feeding level \(f_i(w)\), species interaction matrix \(\theta_{ij}\) prey abundance density \(N_j(w_p)\): $$ D_{ij}(w, w_p) = (1-f_i(w)) \gamma_i(w) \theta_{ij} \int N_j(w_p) \phi_i(w, w_p) w_p dw_p. $$ prey index \(j\) runs species resource. also runs extra ecosystem components model defined encounter rate function. encounter rate multiplied \(1-f_i(w)\) give rate consumption biomass extra components. function performs integration getEncounter() aggregate prey species, multiplies \(1-f_i(w)\) get consumed biomass rather available biomass. Outside range sizes predator species returned rate zero.

title: Get diet of predator at size, resolved by prey species — getDiet
code: diet <- getDiet(NS_params) str(diet) #>  num [1:12, 1:100, 1:14] 8.94e-18 6.86e-19 3.46e-18 1.75e-09 1.12e-17 ... #>  - attr(*, "dimnames")=List of 3 #>   ..$ predator: chr [1:12] "Sprat" "Sandeel" "N.pout" "Herring" ... #>   ..$ w       : chr [1:100] "0.001" "0.00119" "0.00142" "0.0017" ... #>   ..$ prey    : chr [1:14] "Sprat" "Sandeel" "N.pout" "Herring" ...

what: Multiple-resource methods for model rescaling and reporting — params_methods
title: Multiple-resource methods for model rescaling and reporting — params_methods
text: S3 methods make mizer's scaleModel(), scaleRates(), setResource() summary() aware multiple-resource component. base MizerParams methods know single built-resource, setMultipleResources() silences, otherwise ignore (, scaleModel() setResource(), error ) resources stored MR component. Extends mizer::scaleModel() resource carrying capacities abundances resources rescaled consistently. base method scales fish spectra resource abundances (held initial_n_other), resource capacities kappa coefficients, live MR component's parameters. resource replenishment rate left unchanged, exactly built-resource rate base method, steady state preserved. Extends mizer::scaleRates() resource replenishment rate resources rescaled factor along consumer rates. Without base method scale silenced built-resource rate, leaving active resource rates untouched breaking rescaling invariant (resource replenishment keeping pace rescaled search volume). Extends mizer::setResource(). multiple-resource model built-single resource silenced plays part dynamics, changing setResource() effect simulation. method warns user tries change resource rate, capacity level, directs setMultipleResources() related setters. Calls change, example, built-resource dynamics (mizer internally) pass silently. work delegated base method plain class base accessors return (vector-valued) built-resource instead resource--size arrays MR component. Extends summary() method MizerParams objects. base method reports resource size spectrum silenced built-resource, empty multiple-resource model. method instead reports overall size range spanned resources combined.

title: mizerMR 0.3.0
text: Compatible mizer version 3.0.0 setMultipleResources() now uses mizer’s extension-chain methods encounter resource mortality instead replacing entries params@rates_funcs, allowing composition extension packages. Accessors plots need multiple-resource behaviour now registered methods mizer’s generics. plotlySpectra() removed. resource encounter rate now computed single Fourier transform resources combined instead one per resource, encounter cost longer grows number resources. per-resource fallback retained models custom (non-Fourier) predation kernel. scaleModel(), scaleRates(), setResource() summary() now multiple-resource methods. scaleModel() scaleRates() rescale resource capacities, abundances rates consistently (previously scaleModel() errored); setResource() warns affects silenced built-resource; summary() reports combined resource size range instead empty built-resource.

title: mizerMR 0.0.3
text: plotSpectra() plotlySpectra() work multiple resources. New plotting functions plotResourceLevel() plotResourcePred(). setMultipleResources() longer changes initial resource abundances unless supplied via initial_resource. Fix bug preventing resource_params() changing resource arrays. plotDietMR() renamed plotDiet().

title: mizerMR 0.0.2
text: setMultipleResources() now adds extensions field params metadata. plotDietMR() replacement plotDiet() works multiple resources. setMultipleResources()now sets inital_n_ppslot zero spurious contributions getDiet() example. functions extracting information MizerParams MizerSim objects now fall back onto core mizer functions called objects multiple resources set . resource params now saved @other_params slot instead resource_params slot avoid breaking core mizer code.

title: mizerMR 0.0.1
text: First functional version package, ready testing.
